pages.text:
In a world where magic exists much like electricity does in ours, some people choose ordinary lives while others choose dangerous ones.

The dangerous ones are called adventurers, and they are not knights in shining armor or legends carved into history. Most are skilled people who agree to walk into places everyone else avoids. They explore ruins, face strange creatures, and deal with problems too dangerous for towns to handle alone. They are hired help with weapons, training, and very questionable judgment.

The town of Otari is the kind of place that sometimes needs people like that.

Otari sits on the southern coast of an island where a river flows into the sea. About twelve hundred people live there, and most of them make their living the same two ways people have for generations.

They either pull fish out of the water or trees out of the forest.

Logs come down from the hills north of town and are fed into a large waterwheel that powers the sawmills beside the river. Ships wait in the harbor to carry the finished lumber away to bigger cities where someone else will turn it into houses, wagons, or furniture.

The place smells like saltwater, sawdust, and coffee.

It is a working town where people wake early, drink strong coffee, and fix what breaks.

No one here expects adventure. Most people just expect the river to keep flowing, the sawmills to keep turning, and tomorrow to look a lot like today.
